6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AlgClosed.Basic
9	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Geometrically.Integral
10	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ZariskisMainTheorem
11	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Cartesian.Grp_
12	
13	/-!
14	# Abelian varieties
15	
16	## Main results
17	- `AlgebraicGeometry.isCommMonObj_of_isProper_of_geometricallyIntegral`:
18	  A proper geometrically integral group scheme over a field is commutative.
19	
20	-/
21	
22	@[expose] public section
23	
24	open CategoryTheory Limits
25	
26	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
27	
28	universe u
29	
30	variable {K : Type u} [Field K] {X : Scheme.{u}}
31	
32	open MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
33	
34	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
35	instance (G : Over (Spec (.of K))) [GrpObj G] : IsClosedImmersion η[G].left :=
36	  isClosedImmersion_of_comp_eq_id (Y := Spec (.of K)) G.hom η[G].left (by simp)
37	
38	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
39	theorem isCommMonObj_of_isProper_of_isIntegral_tensorObj_of_isAlgClosed [IsAlgClosed K]
40	    (G : Over (Spec (.of K))) [IsProper G.hom] [IsIntegral (G ⊗ G).left] [GrpObj G] :
41	    IsCommMonObj G := by
42	  let S := Spec (.of K)
43	  let point : S := IsLocalRing.closedPoint K
44	  have hpoint : IsClosed {point} := isClosed_discrete _
45	  have : Nonempty G.left := ⟨η[G].left point⟩
46	  have : IsProper (G ⊗ G).hom := by dsimp; infer_instance
47	  have : JacobsonSpace (G ⊗ G).left := LocallyOfFiniteType.jacobsonSpace (Y := Spec _) (G ⊗ G).hom
48	  have : Surjective G.hom := ⟨Function.surjective_to_subsingleton (α := G.left) (β := Spec _) _⟩
49	  have : IsProper (fst G G).left := by dsimp; infer_instance
50	  have : Surjective (fst G G).left := by dsimp; infer_instance
51	  have : IsProper ((GrpObj.commutator G).left ≫ G.hom) := by rw [Over.w]; infer_instance
52	  have : IsClosedImmersion ((lift η[G] η[G]).left ≫ (fst G G).left) := by
53	    simpa using inferInstanceAs (IsClosedImmersion η[G].left)
54	  have : IsClosedImmersion (lift η[G] η[G]).left := .of_comp _ (g := (fst G G).left)
55	  let γ : G ⊗ G ⟶ G ⊗ G := lift (fst _ _) (GrpObj.commutator _)
56	  have : IsProper (γ.left ≫ (fst G G).left) := by simpa [γ]
57	  have : IsProper γ.left := .of_comp _ (fst G G).left
58	  -- It suffices to check that `γ : (x, y) ↦ x * y * x⁻¹ * y⁻¹` is constantly `1`.
59	  rw [isCommMonObj_iff_commutator_eq_toUnit_η]
60	  ext1
61	  have H : γ.left '' ((fst G G).left ⁻¹' {η[G].left point}) ⊆ {(lift η[G] η[G]).left point} := by
62	    rw [Set.image_subset_iff, ← Set.diff_eq_empty, ← Set.not_nonempty_iff_eq_empty]
63	    intro H
64	    obtain ⟨c₀, ⟨hc₁, hc₂⟩, hc₃⟩ := nonempty_inter_closedPoints H <| by
65	      rw [Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, ← Set.image_singleton, ← Set.image_singleton];
66	      refine (IsOpen.isLocallyClosed ?_).inter (IsClosed.isLocallyClosed ?_)
67	      · exact (((lift η[G] η[G]).left.isClosedMap _ hpoint).preimage γ.left.continuous).isOpen_compl
68	      · exact (η[G].left.isClosedMap _ hpoint).preimage (fst G G).left.continuous
69	    obtain ⟨⟨c, hc⟩, e⟩ := (pointEquivClosedPoint (G ⊗ G).hom).surjective ⟨c₀, hc₃⟩
70	    obtain rfl : c point = c₀ := congr(($e).1)
71	    let fc : 𝟙_ (Over S) ⟶ 𝟙_ (Over S) ⊗ G := lift (𝟙 _) (Over.homMk c hc ≫ snd G G)
72	    have : c ≫ pullback.fst G.hom G.hom = η[G].left :=
73	      ext_of_apply_closedPoint_eq G.hom (by simpa) (by simp) (by simpa)
74	    have H₁ : c = fc.left ≫ (η[G] ▷ G).left := by dsimp; ext <;> simp [fc, S, this]
75	    have H₂ : fc ≫ η ▷ G ≫ γ = lift η η := by ext1 <;> simp [fc, γ, S]
76	    exact hc₂ <| by simp [H₁, H₂, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, Category.assoc, ← Over.comp_left]
77	  -- Since the image of `y ↦ γ(e, y)` is finite, by Zariski Main, there exists an open
78	  -- `1 ∈ U ⊆ G` such that `γ ∣_ U` factors through a finite scheme over `U`.
79	  obtain ⟨U, hηU, H⟩ := exists_finite_imageι_comp_morphismRestrict_of_finite_image_preimage
80	    γ.left (fst G G).left (η[G].left point) (by
81	      dsimp [-Scheme.Hom.comp_base, γ]
82	      simp only [pullback.lift_fst]
83	      exact (Set.finite_singleton _).subset H)
84	  have H (x : U) : ((pullback.fst G.hom G.hom) ⁻¹' {x.1} ∩ Set.range ⇑γ.left).Finite := by
85	    refine ((((γ.left.imageι ≫ (fst G G).left) ∣_ U).finite_preimage_singleton x).image
86	      (Scheme.Opens.ι _ ≫ γ.left.imageι)).subset ?_
87	    have : U.ι ⁻¹' {x.1} = {x} := by ext; simp
88	    rw [← this, ← Set.preimage_comp, ← TopCat.coe_comp, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_base,
89	      morphismRestrict_ι, ← Category.assoc, Scheme.Hom.comp_base (_ ≫ _) (fst G G).left,
90	      TopCat.coe_comp, Set.preimage_comp, Set.image_preimage_eq_inter_range]
91	    simp only [Scheme.Hom.comp_base, TopCat.coe_comp, Set.range_comp, Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
92	    dsimp
93	    rw [Set.image_preimage_eq_inter_range, Scheme.IdealSheafData.range_subschemeι,
94	      Scheme.Hom.support_ker, ← Set.inter_assoc, ← Set.preimage_inter,
95	      Set.singleton_inter_of_mem x.2, IsClosed.closure_eq
96	      (by exact γ.left.isClosedMap.isClosed_range)]
97	  -- It suffices to check set-theoretic equality on closed points of `U ×[k] G`.
98	  refine ext_of_apply_eq G.hom _
99	    ((fst G G).left ⁻¹ᵁ U).isOpen.isLocallyClosed
100	    (((fst G G).left ⁻¹ᵁ U).isOpen.dense ?_) ?_ ?_
101	  · exact .preimage ⟨_, hηU⟩ (fst G G).left.surjective
102	  · intro y hyU hy
103	    have hx : IsClosed {(fst G G).left y} := by simpa using (fst G G).left.isClosedMap _ hy
104	    let x : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ G := Over.homMk (pointOfClosedPoint G.hom _ hx) (by simp)
105	    let xe : (G ⊗ G).left := (fst G G ≫ (ρ_ _).inv ≫ G ◁ η[G]).left y
106	    have : γ.left y = xe := by
107	      -- By the choice of `U`, the set `γ({y} ×[k] G)` is finite and hence, by irreducibility,
108	      -- a singleton.
109	      refine subsingleton_image_closure_of_finite_of_isPreirreducible
110	        (hx.preimage (fst G G).left.continuous).isLocallyClosed ?_ γ.left.continuous
111	        γ.left.isClosedMap ((H ⟨_, hyU⟩).subset (Set.image_subset_iff.mpr fun _ ↦ by
112	          simp [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, -Scheme.Hom.comp_base, γ])) ?_ ?_
113	      · let α : G ⊗ G ⟶ G ⊗ G := toUnit _ ≫ x ⊗ₘ 𝟙 _
114	        convert ((IrreducibleSpace.isIrreducible_univ _).image α.left
115	          α.left.continuous.continuousOn).isPreirreducible
116	        rw [Over.tensorHom_left]
117	        simp [Set.range_comp, Scheme.Pullback.range_map, x]
118	      · exact ⟨y, subset_closure (by simp), rfl⟩
119	      · refine ⟨xe, subset_closure ?_, ?_⟩
120	        · simp [xe, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, -Scheme.Hom.comp_base]
121	        · simp only [xe, γ, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ← Over.comp_left]
122	          congr 6; ext <;> simp
123	    convert congr((snd G G).left $this) using 1
124	    · simp [γ, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
125	    · simp [xe, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, -Scheme.Hom.comp_base]
126	  · simp
127	
128	/-- A proper geometrically integral group scheme over a field is commutative. -/
129	@[stacks 0BFD]
130	theorem isCommMonObj_of_isProper_of_geometricallyIntegral
131	    (G : Over (Spec (.of K))) [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIntegral G.hom] [GrpObj G] :
132	    IsCommMonObj G := by
133	  let f := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom <| algebraMap K (AlgebraicClosure K))
134	  let G' := (Over.pullback f).obj G
135	  have : IsProper G'.hom := by dsimp [G']; infer_instance
136	  have : IsIntegral (G' ⊗ G').left := by dsimp [G']; infer_instance
137	  let : GrpObj G' := Functor.grpObjObj
138	  have := isCommMonObj_of_isProper_of_isIntegral_tensorObj_of_isAlgClosed G'
139	  rw [isCommMonObj_iff_commutator_eq_toUnit_η] at this ⊢
140	  apply (Over.pullback f).map_injective
141	  rw [← cancel_epi (Functor.Monoidal.μIso (Over.pullback f) G G).hom]
142	  dsimp [GrpObj.commutator] at this ⊢
143	  simpa only [Functor.map_mul, one_eq_one, comp_one, Functor.map_one, Functor.map_inv',
144	    comp_mul, GrpObj.comp_inv, Functor.Monoidal.μ_fst, Functor.Monoidal.μ_snd]
145	
146	end AlgebraicGeometry

7	
8	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AlgClosed.Basic
9	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth
10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_
11	
12	/-!
13	# Smoothness of group schemes
14	
15	## Main results
16	- `AlgebraicGeometry.smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed`:
17	  If `G` is a group scheme over an algebraically closed field `k` that is reduced and locally
18	  of finite type, then `G` is smooth over `k`.
19	-/
20	
21	@[expose] public section
22	
23	open CategoryTheory
24	
25	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
26	
27	universe u
28	
29	variable {K : Type u} [Field K] [IsAlgClosed K] {G : Scheme} (f : G ⟶ Spec (.of K))
30	    [LocallyOfFinitePresentation f] [IsReduced G] [GrpObj (Over.mk f)]
31	
32	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
33	open MonObj MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory in
34	/--
35	If `G` is a group scheme over an algebraically closed field `k` that is reduced and locally
36	of finite type, then `G` is smooth over `k`.
37	-/
38	lemma smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed : Smooth f := by
39	  have := LocallyOfFiniteType.jacobsonSpace f
40	  have : Nonempty G := ⟨η[Over.mk f].1 (IsLocalRing.closedPoint _)⟩
41	  rw [← Scheme.Hom.smoothLocus_eq_top_iff, ← TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_eq_univ,
42	    ← not_ne_iff, ← Set.nonempty_compl]
43	  intro H
44	  obtain ⟨x, hx, hxc⟩ :=
45	    nonempty_inter_closedPoints H f.smoothLocus.2.isClosed_compl.isLocallyClosed
46	  obtain ⟨y, hy : y ∈ f.smoothLocus, hyc⟩ := nonempty_inter_closedPoints
47	    f.dense_smoothLocus_of_perfectField.nonempty f.smoothLocus.2.isLocallyClosed
48	  let x' : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ Over.mk f := Over.homMk _ ((pointEquivClosedPoint f).symm ⟨x, hxc⟩).2
49	  let y' : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ Over.mk f := Over.homMk _ ((pointEquivClosedPoint f).symm ⟨y, hyc⟩).2
50	  let α := (GrpObj.mulRight (A := Over.mk f) x').symm ≪≫
51	    (GrpObj.mulRight (A := Over.mk f) y')
52	  have hα : x' ≫ α.hom = y' := by
53	    dsimp only [Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom, α]
54	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← Iso.eq_comp_inv]
55	    simp [comp_lift_assoc]
56	  have hα' : α.hom.left x = y := by
57	    simpa [x', y', pointEquivClosedPoint] using congr(($hα).left (IsLocalRing.closedPoint K))
58	  rw! [← hα', ← α.hom.left.mem_preimage, Scheme.Hom.preimage_smoothLocus_eq,
59	    show α.hom.left ≫ f = f from α.hom.w] at hy
60	  exact hx hy
61	
62	end AlgebraicGeometry

7	module

10	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic
11	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ValuativeCriterion

13	/-!
14	# Properness of `Proj A`
15	
16	We show that `Proj 𝒜` is proper over `Spec 𝒜₀`.
17	
18	## Notes
19	This contribution was created as part of the Durham Computational Algebraic Geometry Workshop
20	
21	-/
22	
23	@[expose] public section
24	
25	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Proj
26	
27	variable {σ A : Type*}
28	variable [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
29	variable (𝒜 : ℕ → σ)
30	variable [GradedRing 𝒜]
31	
32	open Scheme CategoryTheory Limits pullback HomogeneousLocalization
33	
34	section IsSeparated
35	
36	lemma lift_awayMapₐ_awayMapₐ_surjective {d e : ℕ} {f : A} (hf : f ∈ 𝒜 d)
37	    {g : A} (hg : g ∈ 𝒜 e) {x : A} (hx : x = f * g) (hd : 0 < d) :
38	    Function.Surjective
39	      (Algebra.TensorProduct.lift (awayMapₐ 𝒜 hg hx) (awayMapₐ 𝒜 hf (hx.trans (mul_comm _ _)))
40	        (fun _ _ ↦ .all _ _)) := by
41	  intro z
42	  obtain ⟨⟨n, ⟨a, ha⟩, ⟨b, hb'⟩, ⟨j, (rfl : _ = b)⟩⟩, rfl⟩ := mk_surjective z
43	  by_cases hfg : (f * g) ^ j = 0
44	  · use 0
45	    have := HomogeneousLocalization.subsingleton 𝒜 (x := Submonoid.powers x) ⟨j, hx ▸ hfg⟩
46	    exact this.elim _ _
47	  have : n = j * (d + e) := by
48	    apply DirectSum.degree_eq_of_mem_mem 𝒜 hb'
49	    · convert SetLike.pow_mem_graded _ _ using 2
50	      · infer_instance
51	      · exact hx ▸ SetLike.mul_mem_graded hf hg
52	    · exact hx ▸ hfg
53	  let x0 : NumDenSameDeg 𝒜 (.powers f) :=
54	  { deg := j * (d * (e + 1))
55	    num := ⟨a * g ^ (j * (d - 1)), by
56	      convert SetLike.mul_mem_graded ha (SetLike.pow_mem_graded _ hg) using 2
57	      rw [this]
58	      cases d
59	      · contradiction
60	      · simp; ring⟩

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2024 Andrew Yang. All rights reserved.

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.MvPolynomial.Monad
9	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Finite
10	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.FinitePresentation
11	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Spectrum.Prime.Polynomial
12	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PullbackCarrier
13	
14	/-!
15	# Affine space
16	
17	## Main definitions
18	
19	- `AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace`: `𝔸(n; S)` is the affine `n`-space over `S`.
20	- `AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.coord`: The standard coordinate functions on the affine space.
21	- `AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.homOfVector`:
22	  The morphism `X ⟶ 𝔸(n; S)` given by a `X ⟶ S` and a choice of `n`-coordinate functions.
23	- `AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.homOverEquiv`:
24	  `S`-morphisms into `Spec 𝔸(n; S)` are equivalent to the choice of `n` global sections.
25	- `AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.SpecIso`: `𝔸(n; Spec R) ≅ Spec R[n]`
26	
27	-/
28	
29	@[expose] public section
30	
31	open CategoryTheory Limits MvPolynomial
32	
33	noncomputable section
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	universe v u
38	
39	variable (n : Type v) (S : Scheme.{max u v})
40	
41	local notation3 "ℤ[" n "]" => CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial n (ULift ℤ))
42	local notation3 "ℤ[" n "].{" u ", " v "}" => CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial n (ULift.{max u v} ℤ))
43	
44	/-- `𝔸(n; S)` is the affine `n`-space over `S`.
45	Note that `n` is an arbitrary index type (e.g. `Fin m`). -/
46	def AffineSpace (n : Type v) (S : Scheme.{max u v}) : Scheme.{max u v} :=
47	  pullback (terminal.from S) (terminal.from (Spec ℤ[n].{u, v}))
48	
49	namespace AffineSpace
50	
51	/-- `𝔸(n; S)` is the affine `n`-space over `S`. -/
52	scoped[AlgebraicGeometry] notation "𝔸(" n "; " S ")" => AffineSpace n S
53	
54	variable {n} in
55	lemma of_mvPolynomial_int_ext {R} {f g : ℤ[n] ⟶ R} (h : ∀ i, f (.X i) = g (.X i)) : f = g := by
56	  suffices f.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.mapEquiv _ ULift.ringEquiv.symm).toRingHom =
57	      g.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.mapEquiv _ ULift.ringEquiv.symm).toRingHom by
58	    ext x
59	    · obtain ⟨x⟩ := x
60	      simpa [-map_intCast, -eq_intCast] using DFunLike.congr_fun this (C x)
61	    · simpa [-map_intCast, -eq_intCast] using DFunLike.congr_fun this (X x)
62	  ext1
63	  · exact RingHom.ext_int _ _
64	  · simpa using h _
65	
66	
67	@[simps -isSimp]
68	instance over : 𝔸(n; S).CanonicallyOver S where
69	  hom := pullback.fst _ _
70	
71	/-- The map from the affine `n`-space over `S` to the integral model `Spec ℤ[n]`. -/
72	def toSpecMvPoly : 𝔸(n; S) ⟶ Spec ℤ[n].{u, v} := pullback.snd _ _
73	
74	variable {X : Scheme.{max u v}}
75	
76	/--
77	Morphisms into `Spec ℤ[n]` are equivalent the choice of `n` global sections.
78	Use `homOverEquiv` instead.
79	-/
80	@[simps]
81	def toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv : (X ⟶ Spec ℤ[n]) ≃ (n → Γ(X, ⊤)) where
82	  toFun f i := f.appTop ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso ℤ[n]).inv (.X i))
83	  invFun v := X.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map
84	    (CommRingCat.ofHom (MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom ((algebraMap ℤ _).comp ULift.ringEquiv.toRingHom) v))
85	  left_inv f := by
86	    apply (ΓSpec.adjunction.homEquiv _ _).symm.injective
87	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj
88	    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_symm_apply, Adjunction.homEquiv_symm_apply]
89	    dsimp
90	    simp only [Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop, Scheme.ΓSpecIso_naturality, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]
91	    apply of_mvPolynomial_int_ext
92	    intro i
93	    rw [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, coe_eval₂Hom, eval₂_X]
94	    rfl
95	  right_inv v := by ext; simp
96	
97	lemma toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv_comp {X Y : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Spec ℤ[n]) (i) :
98	    toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv n (f ≫ g) i = f.appTop (toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv n g i) := rfl
99	
100	variable {n} in
101	/-- The standard coordinates of `𝔸(n; S)`. -/
102	def coord (i : n) : Γ(𝔸(n; S), ⊤) := toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv _ (toSpecMvPoly n S) i
103	
104	section homOfVector
105	
106	variable {n S}
107	
108	/-- The morphism `X ⟶ 𝔸(n; S)` given by a `X ⟶ S` and a choice of `n`-coordinate functions. -/
109	def homOfVector (f : X ⟶ S) (v : n → Γ(X, ⊤)) : X ⟶ 𝔸(n; S) :=
110	  pullback.lift f ((toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv n).symm v) (by simp)
111	
112	variable (f : X ⟶ S) (v : n → Γ(X, ⊤))
113	
114	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
115	lemma homOfVector_over : homOfVector f v ≫ 𝔸(n; S) ↘ S = f :=
116	  pullback.lift_fst _ _ _
117	
118	@[reassoc]
119	lemma homOfVector_toSpecMvPoly :
120	    homOfVector f v ≫ toSpecMvPoly n S = (toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv n).symm v :=

120	    homOfVector f v ≫ toSpecMvPoly n S = (toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv n).symm v :=
121	  pullback.lift_snd _ _ _
122	
123	@[simp]
124	lemma homOfVector_appTop_coord (i) :
125	    (homOfVector f v).appTop (coord S i) = v i := by
126	  rw [coord, ← toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv_comp, homOfVector_toSpecMvPoly,
127	    Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
128	
129	@[ext 1100]
130	lemma hom_ext {f g : X ⟶ 𝔸(n; S)}
131	    (h₁ : f ≫ 𝔸(n; S) ↘ S = g ≫ 𝔸(n; S) ↘ S)
132	    (h₂ : ∀ i, f.appTop (coord S i) = g.appTop (coord S i)) : f = g := by
133	  apply pullback.hom_ext h₁
134	  change f ≫ toSpecMvPoly _ _ = g ≫ toSpecMvPoly _ _
135	  apply (toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv n).injective
136	  ext i
137	  rw [toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv_comp, toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv_comp]
138	  exact h₂ i
139	
140	@[reassoc]
141	lemma comp_homOfVector {X Y : Scheme} (v : n → Γ(Y, ⊤)) (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
142	    f ≫ homOfVector g v = homOfVector (f ≫ g) (f.appTop ∘ v) := by
143	  ext1 <;> simp
144	
145	end homOfVector
146	
147	variable [X.Over S]
148	
149	variable {n}
150	
151	instance (v : n → Γ(X, ⊤)) : (homOfVector (X ↘ S) v).IsOver S where
152	
153	/-- `S`-morphisms into `Spec 𝔸(n; S)` are equivalent to the choice of `n` global sections. -/
154	@[simps]
155	def homOverEquiv : { f : X ⟶ 𝔸(n; S) // f.IsOver S } ≃ (n → Γ(X, ⊤)) where
156	  toFun f i := f.1.appTop (coord S i)
157	  invFun v := ⟨homOfVector (X ↘ S) v, inferInstance⟩
158	  left_inv f := by
159	    ext : 2
160	    · simp [f.2.1]
161	    · rw [homOfVector_appTop_coord]
162	  right_inv v := by ext i; simp [-TopologicalSpace.Opens.map_top, homOfVector_appTop_coord]
163	
164	variable (n) in
165	/--
166	The affine space over an affine base is isomorphic to the spectrum of the polynomial ring.
167	Also see `AffineSpace.SpecIso`.
168	-/
169	@[simps -isSimp hom inv]
170	def isoOfIsAffine [IsAffine S] :
171	    𝔸(n; S) ≅ Spec <| .of <| MvPolynomial n Γ(S, ⊤) where
172	      hom := 𝔸(n; S).toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
173	        (eval₂Hom ((𝔸(n; S) ↘ S).appTop).hom (coord S)))
174	      inv := homOfVector (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom C) ≫ S.isoSpec.inv)
175	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of (MvPolynomial n Γ(S, ⊤)))).inv ∘ MvPolynomial.X)
176	      hom_inv_id := by
177	        ext1
178	        · simp only [Category.assoc, homOfVector_over, Category.id_comp]
179	          rw [← Spec.map_comp_assoc, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, eval₂Hom_comp_C,
180	            CommRingCat.ofHom_hom, ← Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality_assoc]
181	          simp [Scheme.isoSpec]
182	        · simp
183	      inv_hom_id := by
184	        apply ext_of_isAffine
185	        simp only [Scheme.Hom.comp_base, TopologicalSpace.Opens.map_comp_obj,
186	          TopologicalSpace.Opens.map_top, Scheme.Hom.comp_app, Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop,
187	          Scheme.ΓSpecIso_naturality, Category.assoc, Scheme.Hom.id_app, ← Iso.eq_inv_comp,
188	          Category.comp_id]
189	        ext : 1
190	        apply ringHom_ext'
191	        · change _ = (CommRingCat.ofHom C ≫ _).hom
192	          rw [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc, CommRingCat.hom_ofHom, eval₂Hom_comp_C,
193	            ← CommRingCat.hom_comp, ← CommRingCat.hom_ext_iff,
194	            ← cancel_mono (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).hom]
195	          rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, homOfVector_over, Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop]
196	          simp only [Category.assoc, Scheme.ΓSpecIso_naturality, CommRingCat.of_carrier,
197	            ← Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop]
198	          rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop_assoc, Scheme.isoSpec, asIso_inv, IsIso.hom_inv_id]
199	          simp
200	        · intro i
201	          rw [CommRingCat.comp_apply, ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, coe_eval₂Hom]
202	          simp only [eval₂_X]
203	          exact homOfVector_appTop_coord _ _ _
204	
205	#adaptation_note /-- After nightly-2026-02-23 we need this to avoid timeouts. -/
206	@[simp]
207	lemma isoOfIsAffine_hom_appTop [IsAffine S] :
208	    (isoOfIsAffine n S).hom.appTop =
209	      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).hom ≫ CommRingCat.ofHom
210	        (eval₂Hom ((𝔸(n; S) ↘ S).appTop).hom (coord S)) := by
211	  simp [isoOfIsAffine_hom]
212	
213	@[simp]
214	lemma isoOfIsAffine_inv_appTop_coord [IsAffine S] (i) :
215	    (isoOfIsAffine n S).inv.appTop (coord _ i) = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of _)).inv (.X i) :=
216	  homOfVector_appTop_coord _ _ _
217	
218	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
219	lemma isoOfIsAffine_inv_over [IsAffine S] :
220	    (isoOfIsAffine n S).inv ≫ 𝔸(n; S) ↘ S = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom C) ≫ S.isoSpec.inv :=
221	  pullback.lift_fst _ _ _
222	
223	instance [IsAffine S] : IsAffine 𝔸(n; S) := .of_isIso (isoOfIsAffine n S).hom
224	
225	variable (n) in
226	/-- The affine space over an affine base is isomorphic to the spectrum of the polynomial ring. -/
227	def SpecIso (R : CommRingCat.{max u v}) :
228	    𝔸(n; Spec R) ≅ Spec <| .of <| MvPolynomial n R :=
229	  isoOfIsAffine _ _ ≪≫ Scheme.Spec.mapIso (MvPolynomial.mapEquiv _
230	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso R).symm.commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv).toCommRingCatIso.op
231	
232	@[simp]
233	lemma SpecIso_hom_appTop (R : CommRingCat.{max u v}) :
234	    (SpecIso n R).hom.appTop = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).hom ≫
235	      CommRingCat.ofHom (eval₂Hom ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫
236	        (𝔸(n; Spec R) ↘ Spec R).appTop).hom (coord (Spec R))) := by
237	  ext i
238	  simp [SpecIso]

308	chain has three structural pieces:
309	
310	* Step 1 (`shearMulRight`): build the binary-product shear iso
311	  `σ = ⟨pr₁, μ⟩ : G ⊗ G ≅ G ⊗ G` in `Over (Spec k)` from `GrpObj` data only.
312	  This is a categorical statement (no scheme-side content); modelled on

315	
316	* Step 2 (`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_basechange_along_proj_two`):
317	  the load-bearing presheaf-of-modules base-change identity
318	  `Ω_{(G ⊗ G)/G} ≅ pr_2^* Ω_{G/k}` on `(G ⊗ G).left`.
319	  NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (~150–300 LOC); chains
320	  `KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquiv` (algebra side) with
321	  `PresheafOfModules.pullback` / `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` (presheaf side).
322	
323	* Step 3 (`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_restrict_along_identity_section`):

327	-/

329	section ShearIso
330	
331	variable {C : Type*} [Category C] [CartesianMonoidalCategory C]
332	
333	/-- **Binary-product shear isomorphism** for a group object `G` in a Cartesian
334	monoidal category: the map
335	`σ = lift (fst G G) μ : G ⊗ G ⟶ G ⊗ G`, informally `(a, b) ↦ (a, a · b)`,
336	is an isomorphism. The inverse is
337	`σ⁻¹ = lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ)`, informally
338	`(a, b) ↦ (a, a⁻¹ · b)`.
339	
340	This is the binary-product upgrade of
341	`CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_.lean:277-281`),
342	which packages only the one-input version `(· · f)` for a fixed global element
343	`f : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`. Here both factors of `G ⊗ G` are free.
344	
345	Piece (i.b) Step 1 (per `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` Step 1 of
346	`lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises`). NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (no packaged shear
347	iso for binary products in Mathlib; the iter-133 mathlib-analogist verdict
348	Decision 1 prescribes this exact construction). -/
349	@[simps]
350	def shearMulRight (G : C) [GrpObj G] : G ⊗ G ≅ G ⊗ G where
351	  hom := lift (fst G G) μ
352	  inv := lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ)
353	  hom_inv_id := by
354	    -- `(a, b) ↦ (a, a · b) ↦ (a, a⁻¹ · (a · b)) = (a, b)`.
355	    apply CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext
356	    · simp
357	    · -- Goal: `(σ.hom ≫ σ.inv) ≫ snd G G = 𝟙 (G ⊗ G) ≫ snd G G`
358	      rw [Category.id_comp, Category.assoc, lift_snd]
359	      -- Goal: `σ.hom ≫ (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ) = snd G G`
360	      rw [← Category.assoc, comp_lift]
361	      -- Goal: `lift (σ.hom ≫ (fst G G ≫ ι)) (σ.hom ≫ snd G G) ≫ μ = snd G G`
362	      rw [← Category.assoc, lift_fst, lift_snd]
363	      -- Goal: `lift (fst G G ≫ ι) μ ≫ μ = snd G G`
364	      -- Trick: use `lift_lift_assoc` with f := fst ≫ ι, g := fst, h := snd, then
365	      -- close by `lift_comp_inv_left` and `lift_comp_one_left`.
366	      rw [show (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) μ ≫ μ : G ⊗ G ⟶ G) =
367	          lift (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (fst G G) ≫ μ) (snd G G) ≫ μ from by
368	            rw [MonObj.lift_lift_assoc, lift_fst_snd, Category.id_comp],
369	        CategoryTheory.GrpObj.lift_comp_inv_left, MonObj.lift_comp_one_left]
370	  inv_hom_id := by
371	    -- `(a, b) ↦ (a, a⁻¹ · b) ↦ (a, a · (a⁻¹ · b)) = (a, b)`.
372	    apply CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext
373	    · simp
374	    · -- Goal: `(σ.inv ≫ σ.hom) ≫ snd G G = 𝟙 (G ⊗ G) ≫ snd G G`
375	      rw [Category.id_comp, Category.assoc, lift_snd]
376	      -- Goal: `lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ) ≫ μ = snd G G`
377	      -- Apply `lift_lift_assoc` (← direction): the RHS form `lift f (lift g h ≫ μ) ≫ μ`
378	      -- rewrites to `lift (lift f g ≫ μ) h ≫ μ` with f := fst, g := fst ≫ ι, h := snd.
379	      rw [show (lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ) ≫ μ :
380	          G ⊗ G ⟶ G) =
381	          lift (lift (fst G G) (fst G G ≫ ι) ≫ μ) (snd G G) ≫ μ from
382	          (MonObj.lift_lift_assoc (fst G G) (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G)).symm,
383	        CategoryTheory.GrpObj.lift_comp_inv_right,
384	        MonObj.lift_comp_one_left]
385	
386	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
387	lemma shearMulRight_hom_fst (G : C) [GrpObj G] :
388	    (shearMulRight G).hom ≫ fst G G = fst G G := by
389	  simp [shearMulRight]
390	
391	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
392	lemma shearMulRight_hom_snd (G : C) [GrpObj G] :
393	    (shearMulRight G).hom ≫ snd G G = μ := by
394	  simp [shearMulRight]
395	
396	end ShearIso

398	/-! ### Helpers / main lemma for piece (i.b)
399	
400	The remaining declarations of this section state the helper sub-lemmas
401	and main lemma of piece (i.b) at the presheaf-of-modules level. Their
402	bodies are work-in-progress for iter-134+; the proofs decompose
403	along the closure path documented in
404	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § Piece (i.b) +
405	`analogies/mulright-globalises-cotangent.md`.
406	-/
407	
408	/-- **Compatibility morphism of structure presheaves of rings** along a scheme
409	morphism `f`. This is the `φ' = ((adj).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c` shape used by
410	`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf`; we package it once here so the
411	`PresheafOfModules.pullback`-based statements below can refer to it
412	uniformly.
413	
414	Specifically, for a scheme morphism `f : Y ⟶ Z`, this is the adjunction
415	transpose of the structure-sheaf morphism `f.c : O_Z ⟶ f.base _* O_Y`,
416	delivering a morphism `f.base⁻¹ O_Z ⟶ O_Y` of presheaves of rings on
417	`Y`.
418	
419	**Note**: this is **not** the φ for `PresheafOfModules.pullback`, which
420	expects a morphism of presheaves on the *codomain* `Y`
421	(`Y.ringCatSheaf ⟶ (Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf`) and is
422	obtained via `(Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom`. The two conventions
423	serve distinct downstream consumers. -/
424	noncomputable def schemeHomRingCompatibility {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ Z) :
425	    (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback (C := CommRingCat) f.base).obj Z.presheaf ⟶ Y.presheaf :=
426	  ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c
427

12	/-!
13	# Yoneda embedding of `Grp C`
14	
15	We show that group objects are exactly those whose yoneda presheaf is a presheaf of groups,
16	by constructing the yoneda embedding `Grp C ⥤ Cᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{v}` and
17	showing that it is fully faithful and its (essential) image is the representable functors.
18	-/
19	
20	@[expose] public section
21	
22	assert_not_exists Field
23	
24	open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory Limits Opposite CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
25	
26	namespace CategoryTheory
27	universe w v u
28	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] [CartesianMonoidalCategory C]
29	  {M G H X Y : C} [MonObj M] [GrpObj G] [GrpObj H]
30	
31	variable (X) in
32	/-- If `X` represents a presheaf of monoids, then `X` is a monoid object. -/
33	@[to_additive (attr := implicit_reducible)
34	/-- If `X` represents a presheaf of additive monoids, then `X` is an additive monoid object. -/]
35	def GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{w}) (α : (F ⋙ forget _).RepresentableBy X) :
36	    GrpObj X where
37	  __ := MonObj.ofRepresentableBy X (F ⋙ forget₂ GrpCat MonCat) α
38	  inv := α.homEquiv'.symm (α.homEquiv (𝟙 _))⁻¹
39	  left_inv := by
40	    change lift (α.homEquiv'.symm (α.homEquiv (𝟙 X))⁻¹) (𝟙 X) ≫
41	      α.homEquiv'.symm (α.homEquiv' (fst X X) * α.homEquiv' (snd X X)) =
42	        toUnit X ≫ α.homEquiv'.symm 1
43	    apply α.homEquiv'.injective
44	    simp only [α.homEquiv'_comp, Equiv.apply_symm_apply, map_mul, map_one]
45	    simp only [← α.homEquiv'_comp, Functor.comp_obj, lift_fst, Equiv.apply_symm_apply, lift_snd]
46	    exact inv_mul_cancel (α.homEquiv (𝟙 X))
47	  right_inv := by
48	    change lift (𝟙 X) (α.homEquiv'.symm (α.homEquiv' (𝟙 X))⁻¹) ≫
49	      α.homEquiv'.symm (α.homEquiv' (fst X X) * α.homEquiv' (snd X X)) =
50	        toUnit X ≫ α.homEquiv'.symm 1
51	    apply α.homEquiv'.injective
52	    simp only [α.homEquiv'_comp, Equiv.apply_symm_apply, map_mul, map_one]
53	    simp only [← α.homEquiv'_comp]
54	    simp
55	
56	/-- If `G` is a group object, then `Hom(X, G)` has a group structure. -/
57	@[to_additive
58	/-- If `G` is an additive group object, then `Hom(X, G)` has an additive group structure. -/]
59	abbrev Hom.group : Group (X ⟶ G) where
60	  inv f := f ≫ ι
61	  inv_mul_cancel f := calc
62	    lift (f ≫ ι) f ≫ μ
63	    _ = (f ≫ lift ι (𝟙 G)) ≫ μ := by simp
64	    _ = toUnit X ≫ η := by rw [Category.assoc]; simp
65	
66	scoped[CategoryTheory.MonObj] attribute [instance] Hom.group
67	scoped[CategoryTheory.AddMonObj] attribute [instance] Hom.addGroup
68	
69	@[to_additive]
70	lemma Hom.inv_def (f : X ⟶ G) : f⁻¹ = f ≫ ι := rfl
71	
72	variable (G) in
73	/-- If `G` is a group object, then `Hom(-, G)` is a presheaf of groups. -/
74	@[to_additive (attr := simps)
75	/-- If `G` is an additive group object, then `Hom(-, G)` is a presheaf of additive groups. -/]
76	def yonedaGrpObj : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{v} where
77	  obj X := GrpCat.of (unop X ⟶ G)
78	  map φ := GrpCat.ofHom ((yonedaMonObj G).map φ).hom
79	
80	variable (G) in
81	/-- If `G` is a monoid object, then `Hom(-, G)` as a presheaf of monoids is represented by `G`. -/
82	@[to_additive
83	/-- If `G` is an additive monoid object, then `Hom(-, G)` as a presheaf of additive monoids
84	is represented by `G`. -/]
85	def yonedaGrpObjRepresentableBy : (yonedaGrpObj G ⋙ forget _).RepresentableBy G :=
86	  Functor.representableByEquiv.symm (.refl _)
87	
88	variable (G) in
89	@[to_additive]
90	lemma GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy_yonedaGrpObjRepresentableBy :
91	    ofRepresentableBy G _ (yonedaGrpObjRepresentableBy G) = ‹GrpObj G› := by
92	  ext; change lift (fst G G) (snd G G) ≫ μ = μ; rw [lift_fst_snd, Category.id_comp]
93	
94	variable (X) in
95	/-- If `X` represents a presheaf of groups `F`, then `Hom(-, X)` is isomorphic to `F` as
96	a presheaf of groups. -/
97	@[to_additive (attr := simps! hom inv)
98	/-- If `X` represents a presheaf of additive groups `F`, then `Hom(-, X)` is isomorphic to `F` as
99	a presheaf of additive groups. -/]
100	def yonedaGrpObjIsoOfRepresentableBy (F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{v}) (α : (F ⋙ forget _).RepresentableBy X) :
101	    letI := GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy X F α
102	    yonedaGrpObj X ≅ F :=
103	  letI := GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy X F α
104	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun Y ↦ MulEquiv.toGrpIso
105	    { toEquiv := α.homEquiv
106	      map_mul' :=
107	  ((yonedaMonObjIsoOfRepresentableBy X (F ⋙ forget₂ GrpCat MonCat) α).hom.app Y).hom.map_mul })
108	      fun φ ↦ GrpCat.hom_ext <| MonoidHom.ext <| α.homEquiv_comp φ.unop
109	
110	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
111	/-- The yoneda embedding of `Grp C` into presheaves of groups. -/
112	@[to_additive (attr := simps)
113	/-- The yoneda embedding of `AddGrp_C` into presheaves of additive groups. -/]
114	def yonedaGrp : Grp C ⥤ Cᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{v} where
115	  obj G := yonedaGrpObj G.X
116	  map {G H} ψ := { app Y := GrpCat.ofHom ((yonedaMon.map ψ.hom).app Y).hom }
117	
118	@[to_additive (attr := reassoc)]
119	lemma yonedaGrp_naturality (α : yonedaGrpObj G ⟶ yonedaGrpObj H) (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ G) :
120	    α.app _ (f ≫ g) = f ≫ α.app _ g := congr($(α.naturality f.op) g)

12:The genus of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field.
14:## Status (closed iteration 011 — `genus`)
39:/-- The genus of a smooth proper curve. -/
40:noncomputable def genus {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
41:    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
42:    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : ℕ :=

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK
8	
9	/-!
10	# Genus of a smooth proper curve
11	
12	The genus of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field.
13	
14	## Status (closed iteration 011 — `genus`)
15	
16	The iter-011 refactor added the `noncomputable` modifier (user-authorised
17	`2026-05-07`) and an import of `AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK`
18	so the body uses the project's `ModuleCat k`-flavoured cohomology
19	`Scheme.HModule` (iter-009) of the structure sheaf
20	`Scheme.toModuleKSheaf` (iter-006). The body is the probe-confirmed
21	one-liner
22	
23	    Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
24	
25	which is the honest mathematical definition `dim_k H^1(C, O_C)`.
26	
27	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Genus.tex` for the informal proof sketch and
28	`.archon/STRATEGY.md` for the multi-phase build-out plan.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	/-- The genus of a smooth proper curve. -/
40	noncomputable def genus {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
41	    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
42	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : ℕ :=
43	  Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
44	
45	end AlgebraicGeometry

680	    {X Y Z : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
681	    [IsProper X.hom]
682	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (X ⊗ Y).hom]
683	    [LocallyOfFiniteType (X ⊗ Y).hom]
684	    [IsReduced (X ⊗ Y).left]
685	    [IsSeparated Z.hom]
686	    (f : (X ⊗ Y) ⟶ Z)
687	    (x₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ X)
688	    (y₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Y)
689	    (z₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Z)
690	    (_hf : lift (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ y₀) ≫ f = toUnit X ≫ z₀) :
691	    f = lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y) ≫ f := by
692	  -- The gluing step is scheme-level rigidity: two maps out of a geometrically irreducible reduced
693	  -- source into a separated target that agree on a non-empty open agree everywhere. We replicate
694	  -- the argument of `AlgebraicJacobian.Rigidity`'s `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen` inline (that
695	  -- file is *downstream* of this one, so its wrapper is unavailable here) directly from Mathlib's
696	  -- `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'`. The non-empty open and the agreement on it are the
697	  -- genuine geometric content, isolated in `rigidity_eqOn_dense_open`.
698	  obtain ⟨U, hU, h⟩ := rigidity_eqOn_dense_open f x₀ y₀ z₀ _hf
699	  -- `Spec k̄` is a single point, so geometric irreducibility makes `(X ⊗ Y).left` irreducible.
700	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace (X ⊗ Y).left :=
701	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton (X ⊗ Y).hom
702	  -- A non-empty open of an irreducible space is dense, so its inclusion is dominant.
703	  haveI : IsDominant (U.ι : (U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ (X ⊗ Y).left) :=
704	    Scheme.PartialMap.Opens.isDominant_ι (IsOpen.dense U.isOpen hU)
705	  -- Provide separatedness of `Z.left` over `Spec k̄` in the `OverClass.fromOver` form.
706	  haveI : IsSeparated (Z.left ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)) := ‹IsSeparated Z.hom›
707	  -- Promote the underlying-scheme equality to an `Over (Spec k̄)` equality.
708	  refine Over.OverMorphism.ext ?_
709	  exact ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated' (S := Spec (.of kbar))
710	    (X := (X ⊗ Y).left) (Y := Z.left) (f := f.left)
711	    (g := (lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y) ≫ f).left) U.ι h
712	
713	/-- **Rigidity Lemma (Mumford, Form I).** Let `X` be a complete (proper) variety and `Y`, `Z`
714	any varieties over `k̄`. If `f : X ⊗ Y ⟶ Z` collapses the slice `X × {y₀}` to a single point
715	`z₀` (encoded as `lift (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ y₀) ≫ f = toUnit X ≫ z₀`), then `f` factors through
716	the second projection `snd : X ⊗ Y ⟶ Y`, i.e. there is `g : Y ⟶ Z` with `f = snd ≫ g`.
717	
718	This is the cube-free, cohomology-free entry point of the chain: its only inputs are that
719	completeness of `X` makes the projection a closed map, and that a proper connected variety has
720	no nonconstant map to an affine variety. Valid in arbitrary characteristic.
721	
722	SCAFFOLD: signature refined by the iter-157 prover; see blueprint `thm:rigidity_lemma`
723	(Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, Ch. II §4, p. 43).
724	
725	## iter-157 signature correction (the scaffold was false as stated)
726	
727	The iter-157 scaffold carried only `[IsProper X.hom]`. **That statement is false**: take `X` =
728	two disjoint reduced `k̄`-points (proper but disconnected), `Y` = two points, `Z = X`, and
729	`f : X ⊗ Y ⟶ Z` collapsing the `y₀`-fibre to one point while separating the other fibre. Then
730	`_hf` holds yet `f` does not factor through `snd`. Mumford's hypothesis is that `X` is a complete
731	**variety** (irreducible) and `Y`, `Z` are **varieties**; the formal statement therefore needs
732	`X ⊗ Y` geometrically irreducible and reduced (so the dense-open rigidity glue applies) and `Z`
733	separated (so agreement on a dense open propagates). These three instances are added; they are
734	exactly what `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen` (the gluing step) consumes, and what Milne's Rigidity
735	Theorem 1.1 lists ("`V` complete, `V × W` geometrically irreducible, `Z` separated").
736	
737	## iter-157 prover progress
738	
739	The categorical *skeleton* is fully discharged here; only the single geometric core
740	remains. Concretely, Mumford "chooses any point `x₀ ∈ X`" and sets `g(y) = f(x₀, y)`. We
741	make this explicit by adding a `k̄`-point `x₀ : 𝟙_ ⟶ X` to the hypotheses (Mumford's
742	"complete variety `X`" is nonempty, so over `k̄` such a point exists; the downstream
743	consumer `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` supplies it from `ℙ¹`). The witness is then
744	
745	  `g := lift (toUnit Y ≫ x₀) (𝟙 Y) ≫ f`   (the section `y ↦ (x₀, y)` post-composed with `f`).
746	
747	The goal `f = snd X Y ≫ g` rewrites — by pure cartesian-monoidal algebra (`comp_lift`,
748	`toUnit` uniqueness, `Category.comp_id`) — to
749	
750	  `f = retract ≫ f`,   where   `retract := lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y)`
751	
752	is the endomorphism `(x, y) ↦ (x₀, y)` of `X ⊗ Y` that collapses the `X`-axis onto `x₀`.
753	This reduction (lemma `rigidity_snd_lift`, then one `rw`) is closed below. The
754	remaining `f = retract ≫ f` is the genuine geometric heart (`rigidity_core`): `f` depends
755	only on the `Y`-coordinate. Its proof is Mumford's properness/closed-map/affine-constant
756	argument — see `rigidity_core` for the full decomposition into the two char-free bridges.
757	
758	**Status (iter-162)**: PROVEN axiom-clean. Categorical reduction (`rigidity_snd_lift`) and
759	scheme-level gluing (`rigidity_core`, `rigidity_eqOn_dense_open`) all closed; bridge 2
760	(`rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine`) decomposed and assembled — Step 2, Step 1's deep
761	algebra (`eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral`), and Step 1's geometric slice/section assembly
762	(`rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine`, via `isIntegral_of_retract`) all PROVEN. The
763	whole Rigidity-Lemma chain is `sorry`-free. -/
764	theorem rigidity_lemma
765	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
766	    {X Y Z : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
767	    [IsProper X.hom]
768	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (X ⊗ Y).hom]
769	    [LocallyOfFiniteType (X ⊗ Y).hom]
770	    [IsReduced (X ⊗ Y).left]
771	    [IsSeparated Z.hom]
772	    (f : (X ⊗ Y) ⟶ Z)
773	    (x₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ X)
774	    (y₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Y)
775	    (z₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Z)
776	    (_hf : lift (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ y₀) ≫ f = toUnit X ≫ z₀) :
777	    ∃ g : Y ⟶ Z, f = snd X Y ≫ g := by
778	  -- Mumford's witness: `g(y) = f(x₀, y)`, i.e. restrict `f` to the slice `{x₀} × Y`.
779	  refine ⟨lift (toUnit Y ≫ x₀) (𝟙 Y) ≫ f, ?_⟩
780	  -- Reassociate and collapse the projection-then-section composite.
781	  rw [← Category.assoc, rigidity_snd_lift]
782	  -- Goal is now the geometric core `f = retract ≫ f`.
783	  exact rigidity_core f x₀ y₀ z₀ _hf
784	
785	/-! ## The Milne §I.3 chain: additivity and homomorphisms
786	
787	From the proven `rigidity_lemma` we derive the two additive-structure corollaries of Milne §I.1
788	that feed the genus-`0` base case (Route C): the additive decomposition of a morphism out of a
789	product (Corollary 1.5) and the fact that a pointed regular map of abelian varieties is a group
790	homomorphism (Corollary 1.2). Both are cube-free and cohomology-free — pure consequences of the
791	Rigidity Lemma and the `GrpObj`-induced group structure on hom-sets
792	(`CategoryTheory.MonObj.Hom.group`/`Hom.monoid`: `u * v = lift u v ≫ μ`, `u⁻¹ = u ≫ ι`,
793	`(1 : X ⟶ A) = toUnit X ≫ η`). -/
794	
795	/-- **Additive decomposition over a product (Milne Corollary 1.5).** Let `V` be complete (proper)
796	and `V ⊗ W` a variety (geometrically irreducible, reduced, locally of finite type) over an
797	algebraically closed `k̄`, and let `A` be an abelian variety. Then any morphism `h : V ⊗ W ⟶ A`
798	based at the identity (`h(v₀, w₀) = η[A]`, encoded `lift v₀ w₀ ≫ h = η[A]`) decomposes — in the
799	`GrpObj`-induced group on `Hom(V ⊗ W, A)` — as the product of its two axis-restrictions pulled back
800	along the projections:
801	
802	  `h = (p ≫ f) · (q ≫ g)`,
803	
804	where `p = fst V W`, `q = snd V W`, `f = (v ↦ (v, w₀)) ≫ h` is `h|_{V × {w₀}}`, and
805	`g = (w ↦ (v₀, w)) ≫ h` is `h|_{{v₀} × W}`. The operation `·` is the hom-group multiplication
806	(`u * v = lift u v ≫ μ`); the statement does **not** assume `A` commutative.
807	
808	PROOF (Milne, Cor 1.5, §I.1). Form the group difference `φ := h / ((p ≫ f) · (q ≫ g))`. A direct
809	hom-group computation shows `φ` collapses the complete `V`-axis `V × {w₀}` to the identity (this is

810	exactly the `_hf` collapse hypothesis of `rigidity_lemma`, using `h(v₀, w₀) = η[A]`), so by the
811	Rigidity Lemma `φ = q ≫ g'` factors through the second projection; `φ` also vanishes on the
812	section `{v₀} × W` of `q`, forcing `g' = 1`, hence `φ = 1` and `h = (p ≫ f) · (q ≫ g)`. -/
813	theorem hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity
814	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
815	    {V W : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
816	    [IsProper V.hom]
817	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (V ⊗ W).hom]
818	    [LocallyOfFiniteType (V ⊗ W).hom]
819	    [IsReduced (V ⊗ W).left]
820	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
821	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom]
822	    (v₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ V)
823	    (w₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ W)
824	    (h : V ⊗ W ⟶ A)
825	    (hh : lift v₀ w₀ ≫ h = η[A]) :
826	    h = (fst V W ≫ (lift (𝟙 V) (toUnit V ≫ w₀) ≫ h)) *
827	        (snd V W ≫ (lift (toUnit W ≫ v₀) (𝟙 W) ≫ h)) := by
828	  haveI : IsSeparated A.hom := inferInstance
829	  -- Name the two axis-restrictions (folding the goal's RHS).
830	  set f : V ⟶ A := lift (𝟙 V) (toUnit V ≫ w₀) ≫ h with hf
831	  set g : W ⟶ A := lift (toUnit W ≫ v₀) (𝟙 W) ≫ h with hg
832	  -- Projection/section identities for the two slice sections.
833	  have hsVfst : lift (𝟙 V) (toUnit V ≫ w₀) ≫ fst V W = 𝟙 V := by simp
834	  have hsVsnd : lift (𝟙 V) (toUnit V ≫ w₀) ≫ snd V W = toUnit V ≫ w₀ := by simp
835	  have hsWfst : lift (toUnit W ≫ v₀) (𝟙 W) ≫ fst V W = toUnit W ≫ v₀ := by simp
836	  have hsWsnd : lift (toUnit W ≫ v₀) (𝟙 W) ≫ snd V W = 𝟙 W := by simp
837	  -- The cross-restrictions of `h` are the identity, because `h(v₀, w₀) = η[A]`.
838	  have hwsW : w₀ ≫ lift (toUnit W ≫ v₀) (𝟙 W) = lift v₀ w₀ := by
839	    rw [comp_lift, Category.comp_id, ← Category.assoc,
840	      toUnit_unique (w₀ ≫ toUnit W) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
841	  have hvsV : v₀ ≫ lift (𝟙 V) (toUnit V ≫ w₀) = lift v₀ w₀ := by
842	    rw [comp_lift, Category.comp_id, ← Category.assoc,
843	      toUnit_unique (v₀ ≫ toUnit V) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]

847	  set φ : V ⊗ W ⟶ A := h / ((fst V W ≫ f) * (snd V W ≫ g)) with hφ
848	  -- `φ` collapses the complete `V`-axis `V × {w₀}` to the identity.
849	  have hcolV : lift (𝟙 V) (toUnit V ≫ w₀) ≫ φ = toUnit V ≫ η[A] := by
850	    rw [← Hom.one_def, hφ, GrpObj.comp_div, ← hf, MonObj.comp_mul,
851	      ← Category.assoc, hsVfst, Category.id_comp,
852	      ← Category.assoc, hsVsnd, Category.assoc, hwg, ← Hom.one_def, _root_.mul_one, div_self']
853	  -- `φ` vanishes on the `{v₀} × W` section of the second projection.
854	  have hcolW : lift (toUnit W ≫ v₀) (𝟙 W) ≫ φ = (1 : W ⟶ A) := by
855	    rw [hφ, GrpObj.comp_div, ← hg, MonObj.comp_mul,
856	      ← Category.assoc, hsWfst, Category.assoc, hvf, ← Hom.one_def,
857	      ← Category.assoc, hsWsnd, Category.id_comp, _root_.one_mul, div_self']
858	  -- Rigidity: `φ` factors through the second projection.
859	  obtain ⟨g', hg'⟩ := rigidity_lemma φ v₀ w₀ η[A] hcolV
860	  -- The factor is the identity, read off the `{v₀} × W` section.
861	  have hg'1 : g' = 1 := by
862	    have hsec : lift (toUnit W ≫ v₀) (𝟙 W) ≫ φ = g' := by
863	      rw [hg', ← Category.assoc, hsWsnd, Category.id_comp]
864	    rw [← hsec, hcolW]
865	  have hφ1 : φ = 1 := by rw [hg', hg'1, MonObj.comp_one]
866	  -- Conclude `h = f∘p · g∘q`.
867	  have hdiv : h / ((fst V W ≫ f) * (snd V W ≫ g)) = 1 := by rw [← hφ]; exact hφ1
868	  exact div_eq_one.mp hdiv
869	
870	/-- **A pointed regular map of abelian varieties is a homomorphism (Milne Corollary 1.2).** Let
871	`A`, `B` be abelian varieties over an algebraically closed `k̄` (with `A ⊗ A` a variety —
872	geometrically irreducible, reduced, locally of finite type, the hypotheses
873	`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` consumes with `V = W = A`). If a regular map `α : A ⟶ B` sends the
874	identity to the identity (`η[A] ≫ α = η[B]`), then `α` is a monoid (hence group) homomorphism:
875	`IsMonHom α`, i.e. `α(a · a') = α(a) · α(a')`.
876	
877	PROOF (Milne, Cor 1.2, §I.1). Apply the additive decomposition `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`
878	(Cor 1.5) to `h := μ[A] ≫ α : A ⊗ A ⟶ B`, based at `η[A]` (the hypothesis `h(η, η) = η[B]` is
879	`η[A] ≫ α = η[B]` since `η[A]` is the hom-group identity). The two axis-restrictions of `h` both
880	collapse to `α` (by the monoid unit laws `lift_comp_one_right`/`left`: `(a, η) ↦ a` and
881	`(η, a) ↦ a`), so the decomposition reads `μ[A] ≫ α = (fst ≫ α) · (snd ≫ α) = (α ⊗ α) ≫ μ[B]`,
882	which is exactly the `mul_hom` axiom; `one_hom` is the pointed hypothesis. -/
883	theorem av_regularMap_isHom_of_zero
884	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
885	    {A B : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
886	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
887	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (A ⊗ A).hom]
888	    [LocallyOfFiniteType (A ⊗ A).hom]
889	    [IsReduced (A ⊗ A).left]
890	    [GrpObj B] [IsProper B.hom]
891	    (α : A ⟶ B) (hα : η[A] ≫ α = η[B]) :
892	    IsMonHom α := by
893	  -- `η[A]` is the identity of the hom-group `Hom(𝟙_, A)`, so `η[A] · η[A] = η[A]`.
894	  have h1 : (η[A] : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ A) = 1 := by
895	    rw [Hom.one_def, toUnit_unique (toUnit _) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
896	  have hbase : lift η[A] η[A] ≫ μ[A] = η[A] := by
897	    rw [← Hom.mul_def, h1, _root_.mul_one]
898	  -- Corollary 1.5 applied to `h := μ[A] ≫ α` with `V = W = A`, based at `η[A]`.
899	  have key := hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity (V := A) (W := A) (A := B)
900	    η[A] η[A] (μ[A] ≫ α) (by rw [← Category.assoc, hbase, hα])
901	  -- Both axis-restrictions of `μ[A] ≫ α` reduce to `α` (monoid unit laws).
902	  rw [show lift (𝟙 A) (toUnit A ≫ η[A]) ≫ μ[A] ≫ α = α by
903	        rw [← Category.assoc, lift_comp_one_right, Category.id_comp],
904	      show lift (toUnit A ≫ η[A]) (𝟙 A) ≫ μ[A] ≫ α = α by
905	        rw [← Category.assoc, lift_comp_one_left, Category.id_comp]] at key
906	  -- `key : μ[A] ≫ α = (fst A A ≫ α) · (snd A A ≫ α)`; package as `IsMonHom`.
907	  exact { one_hom := hα, mul_hom := by rw [key, Hom.mul_def, lift_fst_comp_snd_comp] }
908

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Andrew Yang. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Andrew Yang
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Geometrically.Reduced
9	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Geometrically.Irreducible
10	
11	/-!
12	# Geometrically Integral Schemes
13	
14	## Main results
15	- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyIntegral`:
16	  We say that morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically integral if for all `Spec K ⟶ Y` with `K`
17	  a field, `X ×[Y] Spec K` is integral.
18	  We also provide the fact that this is stable under base change (`by infer_instance`)
19	- `GeometricallyIntegral.iff_geometricallyIntegral_fiber`:
20	  A scheme is geometrically integral over `S` iff the fibers of all
21	  `s : S` are geometrically integral.
22	- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyIntegral.isIntegral_of_isLocallyNoetherian`:
23	  If `X` is geometrically integral, flat, and universally open (e.g. when over a field),
24	  over an integral locally noetherian scheme, then `X` is also integral.
25	- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyIntegral.isIntegral_of_subsingleton`:
26	  If `X` is geometrically integral over a field, then it is integral.
27	-/
28	
29	@[expose] public section
30	
31	open CategoryTheory MorphismProperty Limits
32	
33	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
34	
35	variable {X Y Z S : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ S) (g : Y ⟶ S)
36	
37	/-- We say that morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically integral if for all `Spec K ⟶ Y` with `K`
38	a field, `X ×[Y] Spec K` is integral. -/
39	@[mk_iff]
40	class GeometricallyIntegral (f : X ⟶ Y) : Prop where
41	  geometrically_isIntegral : geometrically IsIntegral f
42	
43	lemma GeometricallyIntegral.eq_geometrically :
44	    @GeometricallyIntegral = geometrically IsIntegral := by
45	  ext; exact geometricallyIntegral_iff _
46	
47	lemma GeometricallyIntegral.eq_geometricallyReduced_inf_geometricallyIrreducible :
48	    @GeometricallyIntegral =
49	      (@GeometricallyReduced ⊓ @GeometricallyIrreducible : MorphismProperty Scheme) := by
50	  rw [eq_geometrically, GeometricallyReduced.eq_geometrically,
51	    GeometricallyIrreducible.eq_geometrically, ← geometrically_inf]
52	  eta_expand
53	  simp [isIntegral_iff_irreducibleSpace_and_isReduced, and_comm]
54	
55	instance (priority := low) [GeometricallyIntegral f] : GeometricallyReduced f :=
56	  (GeometricallyIntegral.eq_geometricallyReduced_inf_geometricallyIrreducible.le _ _ _ ‹_›).1
57	
58	instance (priority := low) [GeometricallyIntegral f] : GeometricallyIrreducible f :=
59	  (GeometricallyIntegral.eq_geometricallyReduced_inf_geometricallyIrreducible.le _ _ _ ‹_›).2
60	
61	lemma GeometricallyIntegral.of_geometricallyReduced_of_geometricallyIrreducible
62	    [GeometricallyReduced f] [GeometricallyIrreducible f] :
63	    GeometricallyIntegral f :=
64	  GeometricallyIntegral.eq_geometricallyReduced_inf_geometricallyIrreducible.ge _ _ _ ⟨‹_›, ‹_›⟩
65	
66	instance : IsStableUnderBaseChange @GeometricallyIntegral :=
67	  GeometricallyIntegral.eq_geometrically ▸ inferInstance
68	
69	instance [GeometricallyIntegral g] : GeometricallyIntegral (pullback.fst f g) :=
70	  MorphismProperty.pullback_fst f g inferInstance
71	
72	instance [GeometricallyIntegral f] : GeometricallyIntegral (pullback.snd f g) :=
73	  MorphismProperty.pullback_snd f g inferInstance
74	
75	instance (V : S.Opens) [GeometricallyIntegral f] : GeometricallyIntegral (f ∣_ V) :=
76	  MorphismProperty.of_isPullback (isPullback_morphismRestrict ..).flip ‹_›
77	
78	instance (s : S) [GeometricallyIntegral f] :
79	    GeometricallyIntegral (f.fiberToSpecResidueField s) :=
80	  MorphismProperty.pullback_snd _ _ inferInstance

80	  MorphismProperty.pullback_snd _ _ inferInstance
81	
82	instance (s : S) [GeometricallyIntegral f] : IsIntegral (f.fiber s) :=
83	  GeometricallyIntegral.geometrically_isIntegral _ _ _ (.of_hasPullback _ _)
84	
85	instance (priority := low) [GeometricallyIntegral f] : Surjective f :=
86	  ⟨fun x ↦ ⟨_, (f.range_fiberι x).le ⟨Nonempty.some inferInstance, rfl⟩⟩⟩
87	
88	lemma GeometricallyIntegral.isIntegral_of_isLocallyNoetherian
89	    [GeometricallyIntegral f] [Flat f] [UniversallyOpen f]
90	    [IsIntegral S] [IsLocallyNoetherian S] : IsIntegral X := by
91	  rw [isIntegral_iff_irreducibleSpace_and_isReduced]
92	  exact ⟨GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace f f.isOpenMap,
93	    GeometricallyReduced.isReduced_of_flat_of_isLocallyNoetherian f⟩
94	
95	/-- If `X` is geometrically integral over a field, then it is integral. -/
96	lemma GeometricallyIntegral.isIntegral_of_subsingleton
97	    [GeometricallyIntegral f] [Subsingleton S] [IsIntegral S] : IsIntegral X := by
98	  rw [isIntegral_iff_irreducibleSpace_and_isReduced]
99	  refine ⟨GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton f,
100	    GeometricallyReduced.isReduced_of_flat_of_isLocallyNoetherian f⟩
101	
102	/-- If `X` is geometrically integral and flat and universally open over `S` and `Y` is integral
103	and locally noetherian, then `X ×ₛ Y` is integral. -/
104	instance [GeometricallyIntegral f] [Flat f] [UniversallyOpen f] [IsIntegral Y]
105	    [IsLocallyNoetherian Y] : IsIntegral (pullback f g) :=
106	  GeometricallyIntegral.isIntegral_of_isLocallyNoetherian (pullback.snd _ _)
107	
108	instance [GeometricallyIntegral g] [Flat g] [UniversallyOpen g]
109	    [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X] :
110	    IsIntegral (pullback f g) :=
111	  GeometricallyIntegral.isIntegral_of_isLocallyNoetherian (pullback.fst _ _)
112	
113	lemma GeometricallyIntegral.iff_geometricallyIntegral_fiber :
114	    GeometricallyIntegral f ↔ ∀ s, GeometricallyIntegral (f.fiberToSpecResidueField s) := by
115	  simp only [GeometricallyIntegral.eq_geometrically,
116	    ← geometrically_iff_forall_fiberToSpecResidueField]
117	
118	end AlgebraicGeometry

75	`analogies/rigidity-refactor.md`. The declaration was also renamed from
76	`GrpObj.eq_of_eqOnOpen` to `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen`, mirroring
77	Mathlib's `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'` naming idiom
78	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Separated:319–322`).
79	-/
80	
81	/-- Rigidity for morphisms of schemes (scheme-level form): two morphisms
82	`g₁, g₂ : X ⟶ Y` between schemes over `Spec k` whose restrictions to a
83	non-empty open `U ⊆ X` agree as scheme morphisms — for `X` reduced and
84	geometrically irreducible over `Spec k` and `Y` separated over `Spec k` —
85	agree everywhere.
86	
87	This is the `Over (Spec (.of k))`-bundled specialization of Mathlib's
88	`ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'`. The Mumford group-scheme rigidity
89	statement (a morphism vanishing on a non-empty open is zero) is
90	interderivable with this form using the group law on the target. -/
91	theorem Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen
92	    {X Y : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    [IsSeparated Y.hom]
94	    [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
95	    [IsReduced X.left]
96	    (g₁ g₂ : X ⟶ Y) (U : X.left.Opens) (hU : (U : Set X.left).Nonempty)
97	    (h : (U.ι : (U : X.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ X.left) ≫ g₁.left =
98	      (U.ι : (U : X.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ X.left) ≫ g₂.left) :
99	    g₁ = g₂ := by
100	  -- (1) Provide the separatedness instance under the `OverClass.fromOver`
101	  --     form `Y.left ↘ Spec (.of k)` so that the downstream typeclass
102	  --     search at `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'` finds it.
103	  haveI : IsSeparated (Y.left ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)) :=

105	  -- (2) `X.left` is irreducible: `Spec k` is a single point (`Unique`,
106	  --     hence `Subsingleton + Nonempty`), and `GeometricallyIrreducible`
107	  --     over a subsingleton base implies `IrreducibleSpace`.
108	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace X.left :=

110	  -- (3) `U.ι : U.toScheme ⟶ X.left` is dominant: a non-empty open in an
111	  --     irreducible space is dense, and a dense open immersion is dominant.
112	  haveI : IsDominant (U.ι : (U : X.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ X.left) :=
113	    Scheme.PartialMap.Opens.isDominant_ι (IsOpen.dense U.isOpen hU)
114	  -- (4) Promote `g₁ = g₂` from the underlying scheme equality.
115	  --     The `Scheme.Over` and `IsOver` instances for `X.left`, `Y.left`,
116	  --     `g₁.left`, `g₂.left` over `Spec (.of k)` are inferred globally
117	  --     from the `Over (Spec (.of k))` category structure
118	  --     (`OverClass.fromOver`).
119	  refine Over.OverMorphism.ext ?_
120	  exact ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated' (S := Spec (.of k))
121	    (X := X.left) (Y := Y.left) (f := g₁.left) (g := g₂.left) U.ι h

# Analogy: shape of `ProjectiveLineBar`, `Gm`, `Ga`, `gmScalingP1` for the 𝔾ₘ-scaling shortcut

## Mode
api-alignment

## Slug
gm-scaling-p1

## Iteration
165

## Question
What is the Mathlib-aligned shape for the three new infrastructure declarations
`ProjectiveLineBar`, `Gm` (with `Ga` as the demoted-route companion), and
`gmScalingP1 : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`, in particular: which Mathlib API does each
extend (Proj? AffineSpace? a from-scratch Spec? a typeclass? a bare morphism?),
and where should the declarations live?

## Project artifact(s)
- `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:909-989` — three scaffolds (`morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`, `genusZero_curve_iso_P1`, `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme`) consume the to-be-built objects via an *abstract* `P1 : Over (Spec kbar)` proxy.
- `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex:908-989` (def:genus0_base_objects, def:gaTranslationP1) — informal description of the three new objects + chartwise σ_× / σ.

## Mathlib state (verified by LSP + on-disk grep)
- **ℙ¹ as a scheme**: NO `ProjectiveSpace` constructor exists. The canonical idiom is `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj 𝒜` for graded rings (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Scheme`).
  - `IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜)` is shipped (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Proper:366`) when `Algebra.FiniteType (𝒜 0) A`. ⇒ `IsProper ProjectiveLineBar.hom` is FREE on this realization.
  - `Proj.affineOpenCover` (`ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic`) ships the standard `D₊(xᵢ)` chart cover.
  - `Proj.awayι : Spec (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f) ⟶ Proj 𝒜` and `Proj.instIsOpenImmersionAwayι` give the two charts `𝔸¹ = D₊(X)` and `D₊(Y) = ℙ¹ \ {0}` directly.
  - `Smooth`, `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1`, `GeometricallyIrreducible` are NOT shipped on `Proj 𝒜.toSpecZero`. These are project-side proofs.
- **`AffineSpace n S`** (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace:46`) ships:
  - `IsAffine` (when `S` is affine), `IsAffineHom`, `Surjective`, `LocallyOfFinitePresentation` (when `n` finite), `SpecIso n R : 𝔸(n; Spec R) ≅ Spec (CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial n R))`, `homOverEquiv : { f : X ⟶ 𝔸(n; S) // f.IsOver S } ≃ (n → Γ(X, ⊤))`, `homOfVector` (build a morphism from a tuple of global sections).
  - NO `Smooth`/`GeometricallyIrreducible` instances. (Project-side.)
- **Group schemes 𝔾_m / 𝔾_a**: NOT in Mathlib. No `AlgebraicGeometry.Gm`/`Ga` declaration, no `GroupScheme` namespace.
- **`GrpObj` Yoneda installer**: `CategoryTheory.GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat) (α : (F ⋙ forget).RepresentableBy X) : GrpObj X` at `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Cartesian.Grp_:35` — the canonical way to install a group-object instance on a Yoneda-representable presheaf of groups. (Project's `Cotangent/GrpObj.lean` already uses `GrpObj` from this namespace.)
- **Smoothness of GrpObj over k̄**: `AlgebraicGeometry.smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth:38`) — `[GrpObj (Over.mk f)] + [LocallyOfFinitePresentation f] + [IsReduced G] + [IsAlgClosed K] ⇒ Smooth f`. ⇒ For `Gm`/`Ga` as `GrpObj`, `Smooth` is FREE.
- **GrpObj base change**: `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GrpObjAsOverPullback` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Pullbacks:808`) — `GrpObj (asOver M S) ⇒ GrpObj (asOver (pullback (M ↘ S) f) T)`. ⇒ If we ever build `Gm` integrally (over `Spec ℤ`), the `GrpObj` over `Spec k̄` is FREE by pullback. (Out of scope for this iter — just keep the option open.)
- **Glue chartwise morphisms**: `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Gluing:?`) — given a cover `𝒰` of `X` and `f i : 𝒰.X i ⟶ Y` matching on pairwise pullbacks, get `X ⟶ Y`. This is the canonical "define a morphism on each chart" tool.
- **Rigidity glue**: `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Separated:319`) — already used by the project (`AlgebraicJacobian/Rigidity.lean:91`, `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:709`). 𝔾ₘ-density in ℙ¹ via `IsOpen.dense U.isOpen hU` + this lemma is the consumer pattern.

## Decisions identified

### Decision D1: realization of `ProjectiveLineBar`

- **Mathlib idiom**: `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj 𝒜` of a graded ring, specialised to `𝒜 = ℕ → Submodule k̄ (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄)` (the standard ℕ-grading by total degree). The two charts `𝔸¹ = D₊(X₀)` and `ℙ¹ \ {0} = D₊(X₁)` are then `Proj.awayι` images. Cite: `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Scheme:?` (Proj), `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic:?` (Proj.awayι, Proj.affineOpenCover), `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Proper:366` (IsProper).
- **Project's current path**: abstract proxy `P1 : Over (Spec kbar)` with the curve-typeclass bundle `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1, IsProper, GeometricallyIrreducible, genus = 0]`. The proxy is genuinely abstract — there is no concrete underlying scheme.
- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. The abstract proxy *cannot* define a chartwise morphism (`σ_×`) — it has no charts. It also cannot provide an `𝔸¹` chart on which 𝔾ₘ is the dense open subset. So the proxy must be replaced by a concrete construction once we want `gmScalingP1` to exist as a Lean term.
- **Cost of divergence**: the proxy approach forces `gmScalingP1` to remain a `sorry`-stubbed black box; no chartwise definition, no `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` proof. The whole 𝔾ₘ-scaling shortcut becomes unprovable. The Proj realization, by contrast, gives `IsProper` free and gives the two affine charts as actual `Spec` schemes the prover can compute on.
- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — define `ProjectiveLineBar : Over (Spec (.of kbar))` as the base change to `Spec k̄` of `Proj 𝒜` for `𝒜` the standard ℕ-grading on `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` (or pulled back from `Spec ℤ` if upstreaming is in scope). The two charts are then `Proj.awayι 𝒜 X₀ …` and `Proj.awayι 𝒜 X₁ …`, both of which are `Spec` of an explicit `HomogeneousLocalization.Away` — exactly what the chartwise σ_× definition needs.
  - `[IsProper ProjectiveLineBar.hom]` — FREE (`IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜)` for `Algebra.FiniteType k̄ k̄[X₀,X₁]`).
  - `[GeometricallyIrreducible ProjectiveLineBar.hom]`, `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 _]`: project-side sub-proofs. Both are sub-builds (no Mathlib instance for `Proj`). Marked `NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL` *inside* this `ALIGN` verdict.
  - `genus ProjectiveLineBar = 0` — still blocked on Riemann–Roch (same as the proxy path), but at least the statement is concrete.

### Decision D2: realization of `Gm` and `Ga` as group objects

- **Mathlib idiom**: install `GrpObj` via `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` from `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Cartesian.Grp_:35`, presenting the Yoneda-functor of groups (units / additive group of global sections). Use Mathlib's `AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec k̄)` for the underlying scheme of `Ga`, and `Spec (.of (Localization.Away (X : MvPolynomial Unit k̄)))` (= `Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]`) for `Gm`. Use `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth:38`) to derive `Smooth` for free.
- **Project's current path**: not yet built — the blueprint says `AffineSpace 𝔸(1; Spec k̄)` for `Ga` and "𝔾_m = 𝔸¹ \ {0}" for `Gm`, both with a `GrpObj` instance. No body yet.
- **Gap**: in-proposal — there is no shipped code to compare against. The blueprint's *idea* (use `AffineSpace` for `Ga`, the basic-open of `𝔸¹` for `Gm`) is the right *direction* but has two sub-decisions where Mathlib has a clear preferred shape:
  1. **Direct vs. Yoneda installation of `GrpObj`.** Mathlib's idiom (used internally for the projection of representability) is `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy`. The blueprint isn't explicit about which approach. ⇒ pick `ofRepresentableBy`.
  2. **`Gm` as `(AffineSpace _).basicOpen (coord _ 0)` vs. `Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]`.** Both are isomorphic schemes; only the second is *directly affine*. The second is far cleaner for installing `GrpObj` (it has `IsAffine` for free; basic-opens carry over the structure-sheaf API via `IsLocalization.Away` but are not affine by definition in Mathlib's API).
- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL** (Mathlib doesn't ship these), with two **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** sub-rules:
  - **D2.a — `Ga`**: define `Ga : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) := (AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))` (or the canonical Over via `AffineSpace.over`). Install `GrpObj Ga` via `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with the additive-group functor `(T ↦ AddGrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤))`, using `AffineSpace.homOverEquiv` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace:155`) as the representable-by witness (S-morphisms `T ⟶ 𝔸(1; S)` ≃ `Γ(T, ⊤)`). DO NOT define the multiplication by `Pi.lift` of an ad-hoc `homOfVector` — that builds a parallel API. The Yoneda installer is cheaper and composes with the rest of Mathlib's `Hom.group` API.
  - **D2.b — `Gm`**: define `Gm : Over (Spec (.of kbar))` as the canonical Over of `Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (X : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)))` — i.e. `Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]`. Install `GrpObj Gm` via `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with the multiplicative-group functor `(T ↦ GrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ)`, the representable-by witness being the standard "morphism into Spec of away-localization ↔ unit in global sections" bijection. (Mathlib has `IsLocalization.Away f Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)` at `AffineScheme:632/651/666` and `Spec_basicOpen` at `AffineScheme:596` — these supply the bijection.) DO NOT define `Gm` as `(AffineSpace _).basicOpen _` — the basic-open path has weaker `IsAffine`/structure-sheaf instances and forces bridge lemmas at every consumer site.
  - **D2.c — `Smooth`**: both `Gm.hom` and `Ga.hom` should derive `Smooth` from `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed`. Project responsibility is to provide `[LocallyOfFinitePresentation _.hom]` (free for `Ga` from `AffineSpace.instLocallyOfFinitePresentation…OfFinite`; for `Gm` from `IsAffineHom` + `Algebra.FinitePresentation k̄ k̄[t,t⁻¹]`) and `[IsReduced _.left]` (free — both `MvPolynomial Unit k̄` and `Localization.Away t` are domains over a field).
  - **D2.d — `GeometricallyIrreducible`**: again project-side. Both are `Spec` of an integral domain over `k̄` and base-change to `Spec k̄` is itself (`k̄ = k̄`), so `GeometricallyIntegral` reduces to `IsIntegral` on global sections; conclude via `GeometricallyIntegral → GeometricallyIrreducible` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Geometrically.Integral:58`).

### Decision D3: shape of `gmScalingP1`

- **Mathlib idiom**: bare morphism `gmScalingP1 : ProjectiveLineBar ⊗ Gm ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar` in `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`, defined chartwise by `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Gluing:?`). No `IsAction` / `MulAction` typeclass exists at scheme level in Mathlib — every scheme-level "action" the library knows is a bare morphism plus named property lemmas. Cite as precedent: `GrpObj.mulRight` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Cartesian.Grp_:?`) — a one-sided multiplication packaged as a morphism, no typeclass wrapper.
- **Project's current path**: not yet built — the blueprint description is chartwise (`(x, λ) ↦ λx` on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m`; `1/(λx) = u/λ` near `∞`), with `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` highlighted as the load-bearing property.
- **Gap**: in-proposal. The risk is over-engineering — wrapping σ_× in a typeclass / `MulAction`-style structure that doesn't exist anywhere else in Mathlib.
- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — define `gmScalingP1` as a *bare* `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`-morphism. Expose the load-bearing property as a separate lemma (named e.g. `gmScalingP1_zero_fixed` or `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`):
  ```lean
  -- The fixed-point lemma that the rigidity consumer needs:
  -- σ_×(0, ·) = const 0, packaged as the W-axis collapse hypothesis
  -- of `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`:
  lemma gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero :
      lift (𝟙 ProjectiveLineBar)
        (toUnit ProjectiveLineBar ≫ (zeroPoint : 𝟙_ ⟶ Gm)) ≫
        ... = ... := by ...
  ```
  Use `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` over the two `Proj.awayι`-charts × `Gm` (which together cover `ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m` because `Proj.affineOpenCover` does). Each chart-restriction is then a `Spec.map` of an explicit ring map between `k̄[t, λ, λ⁻¹]`-algebras (on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m`: `t ↦ λ·t`; near `∞`: `u ↦ u/λ`). DO NOT introduce a custom typeclass `class IsSchemeMulAction (G H : ...) ...` — there is no Mathlib precedent and the only consumer needs only the bare morphism + the one fixed-point property.
  - The `Gluing.glueMorphisms` precondition (agreement on pairwise pullbacks) reduces to an equality of `Spec.map`s on the localization `k̄[t, t⁻¹, λ, λ⁻¹]` — a direct ring-level computation.
  - The "𝔾_m as both source and target factor" — only on the source. On the target (ℙ¹) we just hit the chart we like.

### Decision D4: file location

- **Mathlib idiom**: a small, focused scheme construction with one main downstream consumer gets a short file directly under that consumer. Closest precedent: `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth` (62 lines), `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Abelian` (147 lines) — each defines a new instance/lemma in a focused file imported by exactly one or two downstream proofs.
- **Project's current path**: AVR.lean is already 992 lines and the new infrastructure (definitions, 4–5 instances, glue lemmas, the chartwise σ_× construction) is ~150–400 LOC.
- **Gap**: would not break correctness either way, but inlining 250+ LOC of new scheme infrastructure into the rigidity-proof file makes both the proofs and the infrastructure harder to read and harder to upstream later.
- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — split into a new file `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean`, imported by AVR.lean. Keep `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` / `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` / `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` in AVR.lean; move `ProjectiveLineBar`, `Ga`, `Gm`, `gmScalingP1`, and their typeclass instances + chart-level helper lemmas to the new file. Rationale: matches Mathlib's "one focused file per scheme construction with ~one downstream consumer" pattern, keeps AVR.lean's rigidity-proof content centred, and leaves a clean unit for upstream PR'ing later (e.g. as `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpace.One` / `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Gm` / `…Group.Ga`).

## Recommendation

Land the scaffold this iter as:

```text
AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean       -- NEW FILE
  - import AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity-prerequisites
    (Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Proper,
     Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace,
     Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth,
     Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Cartesian.Grp_)
  - namespace AlgebraicGeometry
  - def standardGrading : ℕ → Submodule k̄ (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄)
    + GradedRing instance (project-side, but a routine MvPolynomial homogeneous-decomposition)
  - def ProjectiveLineBar : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) := … (Proj-based)
    instance [IsAlgClosed kbar] : IsProper ProjectiveLineBar.hom := … -- FREE from ProjectiveSpectrum.Proper
    instance : GeometricallyIrreducible ProjectiveLineBar.hom := sorry
    instance : SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 ProjectiveLineBar.hom := sorry
    def zeroPt, onePt, inftyPt : 𝟙_ ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar := … (Proj.awayι-image of the standard k̄-points)
  - def Ga : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) := AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) |>.asOver _
    instance : GrpObj Ga := GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy Ga (… additive functor …) (… via AffineSpace.homOverEquiv …)
    instance : Smooth Ga.hom := smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed _ (instances inferred)
  - def Gm : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) := (Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (X : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)))).asOver _
    instance : GrpObj Gm := GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy Gm (… units functor …) (… via Spec.map-of-away ↔ unit …)
    instance : Smooth Gm.hom := smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed _ (instances inferred)
  - def gmScalingP1 : ProjectiveLineBar ⊗ Gm ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar := … (Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms over the two Proj.awayι charts × Gm)
  - lemma gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero : lift (toUnit (… ≫ zeroPt)) (𝟙 Gm) ≫ gmScalingP1 = toUnit Gm ≫ zeroPt := …
  - (optional, if/when needed) gmScalingP1_action_id : lift (𝟙 ProjectiveLineBar) (toUnit _ ≫ onePt_of_Gm) ≫ gmScalingP1 = 𝟙 ProjectiveLineBar
end AlgebraicGeometry

AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean
  import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects   -- NEW
  -- morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const, genusZero_curve_iso_P1, rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme
  -- are refactored to take the concrete `ProjectiveLineBar` (no more abstract `(P1 : Over …)` proxy)
```

The four big shape rules — **(1)** Proj-based ℙ¹ (not abstract proxy, not glued-from-charts directly), **(2)** Yoneda-installed GrpObj via `ofRepresentableBy` (not direct `μ`-by-hand), **(3)** Spec(`k̄[t,t⁻¹]`) for Gm (not basic-open of `𝔸¹`), **(4)** bare morphism for σ_× (not typeclass) — together remove every parallel-API risk the iter-164 progress critic flagged. The infrastructure built this way also stays directly upstream-able to Mathlib later (no `archon-`-specific wrappers to peel off).

The work the project still owes regardless of this choice — and which `NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL` for `Proj 𝒜`-side: `GeometricallyIrreducible` and `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` of `Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜.hom` for `𝒜` = standard ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`, and `genus ProjectiveLineBar = 0` (Riemann–Roch debt; same on every realization).
